brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day. that means she uses 1/2 ounce in a week. since there are 52 weeks in a leap year, she will use 26 ounces of shampoo in total. each 14-ounce bottle contains 14 ounces of shampoo. so, she needs 2 bottles to get through the year. this calculation assumes that she uses the shampoo consistently and that there are no additional uses.

the number of bottles needed is 2. this is based on the assumption that she uses 1/2 ounce of shampoo per week and that the shampoo is used every other day. the calculation is 52 weeks × 1/2 ounce = 26 ounces. each bottle is 14 ounces, so 26 ÷ 14 = 2. this is a simple calculation that assumes no additional uses or waste. it is important to note that this is a calculation based on the given information and does not account for any other factors that may affect the total amount of shampoo needed.
